title: HUMAN REPRODUCTION - 211info
text: GRANT COUNTY BEHAVIORAL HEALTH HUMAN REPRODUCTION Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF The Oregon Reproductive Health (RH) Program oversees a statewide network of certified health care providers to ensure access to a suite of reproductive health services (including preventive reproductive health care, preconception and contraception). Services include annual gynecological exams (including pap tests, breast exams and STI screening); pregnancy testing; birth control counseling, contraception, emergency contraception, abortion referrals; and more. 528 East Main Street Suite W John Day, OR 97845 Eligibility: Individuals with a household income below 250% FPL Services are provided to all individuals regardless of ability to pay, race, color, national origin, immigration status, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, age, or disability. Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm (541) 575-0429 Get Directions Visit Website Phone/FAX Numbers Main phone (541) 575-0429 INTAKE PROCEDURE: Call for information and to schedule an appointment FEES: None for individuals with a household income under 250% FPL. Sliding scale fees available for individuals with a household income above 250% FPL. Insurance will be billed, if applicable DOCUMENTS: None required at intake AREA SERVED: Grant County Services: Abortion Referrals Birth Control Counseling Breast Examinations Contraception Emergency Contraception General Sexuality/Reproductive Health Education Pap Tests Pregnancy Testing Sexually Transmitted Infection Screening Other Services Offered At This Location: COUNSELING SETTINGS - MENTAL HEALTH SERVICES REHABILITATION/HABILITATION SERVICES - COMMUNITY DEVELOPMENTAL DISABILITIES PROGRAM SUBSTANCE USE DISORDER SERVICES - DRUG AND ALCOHOL TREATMENT If you represent this agency, update your listing here . * The community resource directory information is up to date to the best of our knowledge. However, you should always call the provider to confirm this information and make an appointment. Be sure to confirm payment information with the provider, if payment is required. 211Info does not rate, recommend or endorse any agency. We simply provide information as a public service. GRANT COUNTY BEHAVIORAL HEALTH
